name: NEAR Protocol RPC API
what_it_does: L1 blockchain JSON-RPC API for the NEAR Protocol — a sharded, PoS blockchain focused on developer and user experience. API enables reading chain state, querying accounts, submitting transactions, and interacting with smart contracts.
use_cases: Reading NEAR account balances and on-chain state for DeFi automation agents
Submitting transactions for token transfers, staking, and smart contract calls
Querying contract view methods without gas cost for read-only operations
Monitoring validator and staking pool performance for delegation agents
Building cross-chain bridge monitoring agents via NEAR's Rainbow Bridge
not_for: EVM-native applications — NEAR uses WebAssembly (Rust/TypeScript), not Solidity
Organizations requiring battle-tested 10+ year track record (Ethereum/Bitcoin)
High-frequency trading requiring ultra-low latency finality
best_when: An agent works within the NEAR ecosystem for DeFi, NFTs, or dApp automation with Rust/TypeScript smart contract context.
avoid_when: You need EVM compatibility and a larger DeFi ecosystem — use Ethereum or Polygon instead.

auth.notes: Public RPC nodes available without auth. For production, dedicated providers (NEAR RPC from Pagoda/NEAR Foundation, QuickNode) offer API key access with higher rate limits.

pricing.free_tier_limits: NEAR Foundation public RPC: free with rate limits
pricing.paid_tiers: Pagoda API (NEAR's official provider): tiered plans; QuickNode from $49/month
pricing.notes: NEAR Foundation offers free public RPC access. Pagoda (NEAR's developer platform) provides enhanced RPC with analytics. Multiple third-party providers available.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: NEAR docs are developer-friendly with good SDK examples. JSON-RPC error codes documented. Contract view calls return typed results. Execution receipts provide detailed transaction outcomes including failure reasons.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: NEAR transactions use nonces for deduplication — same nonce cannot be reused. View calls are inherently idempotent. Access key nonces must be managed carefully.
known_agent_gotchas: Access key nonce must be incremented for each transaction — nonce management critical
Function call gas limits must be estimated — insufficient gas causes transaction failure
Transaction receipts are async — transaction submission and execution are separate steps
NEAR accounts use human-readable IDs (alice.near) not hex addresses — different UX model

security_score_components.security_notes: Decentralized blockchain. Access keys have permission levels (FullAccess vs FunctionCall). Private keys never transmitted. Rust-based core implementation. NEAR Foundation security audits published.
